title: Radiant Uruguayan White Bean Stew
description: A fresh, snappy one-pot stew that showcases brine-dried white beans with smoky ham and aromatic spices, delivering a uniquely Uruguayan taste. It is keto-friendly and ready in about two hours.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add diced onion and sauté until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Stir in minced garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add smoked paprika and cumin, tossing to coat the aromatics.
Place the brine-dried beans, smoked ham hock, and bay leaf into the pot.
Pour in 4 cups of water or low-sodium broth, ensuring the beans are fully submerged.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer, partially covered, for 90 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Remove the ham hock, shred the meat, and return it to the pot; discard the hock bones.
Season the stew with salt and pepper to taste, and let it simmer for an additional 10 minutes.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley before serving.
